summary: easy-string-kit postinstall exfiltrates EC2 instance metadata to attacker-controlled oastify.com subdomain
details: package.json declares a `postinstall` lifecycle script that auto-executes on `npm install` and issues 25+ `curl -X POST` requests sending the output of `ls -la /data/<path>` for EC2 instance-metadata-style paths (ami-id, iam, identity-credentials, instance-id, hostname, public-keys, security-groups, etc.) to a hardcoded attacker subdomain `3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com` (Burp Collaborator out-of-band exfiltration infrastructure) over plaintext HTTP. The package presents itself as a generic string-utility kit but ships no string-handling functionality consistent with that purpose; author, repository, bugs, and homepage metadata are all empty. The host-metadata probing has no relationship to the advertised purpose and is unambiguous supply-chain reconnaissance / exfiltration directed at installer infrastructure (in particular AWS EC2 build environments where IMDS-adjacent paths may leak IAM credentials and identity data).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
